claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 18 years old, eligibility for Child Benefit depends on whether they are in approved education or training. You can normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training.

Could you tell me if Alex is currently in approved education or training (such as A-levels, a BTEC, or similar qualifications)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education. He's studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. A-levels count as approved education, so that's a positive sign for eligibility.

Now, could you confirm whether you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

Next, could you tell me about your living arrangements with Alex? Does Alex live with you, or do they live somewhere else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

1. Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

Is Alex working 24 hours or more per week, or receiving any benefits in their own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working that much - just focuses on studying really. And no, they don't receive any benefits in their own right.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**. Here's a summary of why:

✅ **You live in the UK** (Manchester)

✅ **You are responsible for Alex** – he lives with you at home

✅ **Alex is under 20 and in approved education** – A-levels count as approved education, so even though Alex is 18, you can continue to receive Child Benefit while he remains in this education

✅ **No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex**

✅ **Alex is not working 24+ hours per week** and is not receiving benefits in his own right

You should be able to claim or continue receiving Child Benefit for Alex until he finishes his A-levels, or until he turns 20 – whichever comes first.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old, which is over 16 but under 20. For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit can continue if the child remains in approved education or training. Alex is studying for A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and Alex lives at home with them, meaning the claimant is responsible for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not receiving any benefits in their own right. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant appears to be eligible for Child Benefit for their child Alex. They live in the UK, are responsible for Alex (who lives with them), and Alex is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels). There are no disqualifying factors such as another person claiming for Alex, or Alex working excessive hours or receiving their own benefits.

Alex is studying for A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and Alex lives at home with them, meaning the claimant is responsible for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not receiving any benefits in their own right. All eligibility criteria are met.

They live in the UK, are responsible for Alex (who lives with them), and Alex is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels). There are no disqualifying factors such as another person claiming for Alex, or Alex working excessive hours or receiving their own benefits.
